name: akatosh

metadata.name: Akatosh
metadata.summary: Discrete event simulation framework supports real-time simulation without time step!

documentation:
# Akatosh

`Akatosh` is a light-weighted disceret event simulation library. Unlike popular library `Simpy` which is progress-oriented and you have to write generator function for simulated events or events interaction, `Akatosh` is fully object-oriented that events are encapsulated as `InstantEvent`/`ContinousEvent` with states, priority and a life-cycle. The actual impact of events are simply regular python functions. You could create events all at once, or create event within event. In addition, `Akatosh` is async which means event that are happening at the same simulated time will be executed simultaneously for real, unless they have different priority.

`Akatosh` also support `Resource`, provide all functionalities as it is in `Simpy` with extra utilities and interaction with `Entity`. The `Entity` is unique to `Akatosh` which represents a abstract entity with a life-cycle, for example a follower. The `Entity` supports utility functions to interact with `Resource` and automatically releases all its occupied resources upon termination.

You probably already noticed that `Akatosh` is the name of "Dragon God of Time" in elder scroll serie, therefore the singleton class `Mundus` is the core of the simulation. The `Mundus` will schedule the events, move forward time and engage async execution.

This work is licensed under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. To view a copy of this license, visit <https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/>

To use `Akatosh`:

```bash
pip install -U Akatosh
```

A basic example is showing below, for more information please look at *Examples* and *API Reference*, full documentation is available at https://ulfaric.github.io/Akatosh/.

```py
from Akatosh.universe import universe
from Akatosh.resource import Resource
from Akatosh.entity import Entity
from Akatosh.event import Event

# create a resource with capacity of 100 and current level 50
res = Resource(100.0, 50.0)

# create a instance event happens at 0.9s
lock = Event(0.9,0.9, lambda: print("Unlocked!"))

# indicate a user entity should be created after lock event ended, wait till 1.1s.
user = Entity(lock, 1.1, "User")

# indicate user entity engage a event at 1.2s
@user.event(1.2,1.2, "Use Resource")
def user_event():
    if res.distribute(user, 1):
        print(res.level)
    else:
        print("Not enough resource")


# run simulation for 1.2s
universe.simulate(1.2)
```

## Update Log
### 3.1.5
- Fix the entity event creation bug.

### 3.1.4
- Fix the logger format.

### 3.1.3
- Remove time scale feature due to its complexity and issue for causing time desynchronization.
- Rewrite the Mundus time property for better reliability in real-time mode.
- Change to IEC 61131-3 standard implementation:
  - Event will default to stop if deadline is exceeded and no watchdog function is provided.
  - Event will not stop if watchdog function is provided, unless the watchdog cancel or end the event.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.__init__
documentation: The simulation universe.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.simulate
documentation: Simulate the universe until the given time.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.enable_realtime
documentation: Enable the real time simulation.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.disable_realtime
documentation: Disable the real time simulation.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.pause
documentation: Pause the simulation.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.resume
documentation: Resume the simulation.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.set_logging_level
documentation: Set the logging level. Default is DEBUG.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.time
documentation: Return the current time.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.time_resolution
documentation: Return the time resolution. 1 for 0.1s, 2 for 0.01s, 3 for 0.001s, and so on. Default is 3.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.time_resolution
documentation: Set the time resolution. 1 for 0.1s, 2 for 0.01s, 3 for 0.001s, and so on. Default is 3.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.time_step
documentation: The time step of the simulation. Default is 0.001s.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.realtime
documentation: Return True if the simulation is in real time mode, otherwise False. Default is False.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.pending_events
documentation: The events that are pending to be executed. Please note that this is not the queue for future events. This is used for start async tasks for the events.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.current_event_priority
documentation: The current event priority.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.max_event_priority
documentation: The maximum event priority.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.simulation_start_time
documentation: The time when the simulation started.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.simulation_end_time
documentation: The time when the simulation ended.

methods.name: Akatosh.universe.Universe.paused
documentation: Return True if the simulation is paused, otherwise False.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.__init__
documentation:
Create an entity with a creation and termination event.

Args:
    at (float | Event): when the entity is created.
    till (float | Event): when the entity is terminated.
    label (Optional[str], optional): short description to the entity. Defaults to None.
    priority (int, optional): the priority of the entity, only impacts the creation and termination event. Defaults to 0.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.__str__
documentation: Return the label of the entity if it exists, otherwise return the id of the entity.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity._create
documentation: Called when the entity is created.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity._terminate
documentation: Called when the entity is terminated.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.event
documentation: Decorator to add an event to the entity.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.acquire
documentation: Acquire a amount from the resource.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.release
documentation: Release a amount from the resource.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.label
documentation: Short description of the entity.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.created
documentation: Return True if the entity is created, otherwise False.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.terminated
documentation: Return True if the entity is terminated, otherwise False.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.events
documentation: The events that the entity is engaged with.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.occupied_resources
documentation: The resources that the entity is using.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.priority
documentation: The priority of the entity.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.creation
documentation: The creation event of the entity.

methods.name: Akatosh.entity.Entity.termination
documentation: The termination event of the entity.

methods.name: Akatosh.resource.Resource.__init__
documentation:
Create a resource with a given capacity and initial usage.

Args:
    capacity (float): the maximum amount of resource that can be stored.
    usage (float, optional): the initial usage of the resource. Defaults to 0.0.

methods.name: Akatosh.resource.Resource.distribute
documentation: Distribute the given amount of resource to the user.

methods.name: Akatosh.resource.Resource.collect
documentation: Collect the resource from the user. If the amount is infinite, the user will be removed from the users list and all amount will be collected.

methods.name: Akatosh.resource.Resource.reset
documentation: Reset the resource level and users.

methods.name: Akatosh.resource.Resource.capacity
documentation: The maximum amount of resource that can be stored.

methods.name: Akatosh.resource.Resource.usage
documentation: The current usage of the resource.

methods.name: Akatosh.resource.Resource.level
documentation: The current level of the resource.

methods.name: Akatosh.resource.Resource.users
documentation: The users of the resource. Each user is a tuple of the entity and the amount of resource used by the entity.

classes.name: Akatosh.event.IEC61131Exception
documentation: Exception raised when an event exceeds its deadline.

classes.name: Akatosh.event.ExceedWaitingTime
documentation: Exception raised when an event exceeds its waiting time.

classes.name: Akatosh.event.ExceedExecutionTime
documentation: Exception raised when an event exceeds its execution time.

classes.name: Akatosh.event.ExceedEventDuration
documentation: Exception raised when an event exceeds its duration.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.__init__
documentation:
Create an event which happens at a certain time and ends at a certain time.

Args:
    at (float | Event): when the event should start.
    till (float | Event): when the event should end.
    action (Callable): what happens during the event.
    label (Optional[str], optional): Short description for the event. Defaults to None.
    once (bool, optional): whether this event should only happen once, regardless of at or till. Defaults to False.
    priority (int, optional): the priority of the event, event with lower value will happen before the events with a higher priority value. Defaults to 0.

Raises:
    ValueError: _description_

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.__call__
documentation: Make the event callable, so it can be awaited like a coroutine.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.__str__
documentation: Return the label of the event if it has one, otherwise return the id of the event.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.end
documentation: End the event.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.cancel
documentation: Cancel the event.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.pause
documentation: Pause the event.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.resume
documentation: Resume the event.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.at
documentation: Return the time when the event should start.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.till
documentation: Return the time when the event should end.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.started
documentation: Return whether the event has started or not.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.ended
documentation: Return whether the event has ended or not.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.cancelled
documentation: Return whether the event has been cancelled or not.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.paused
documentation: Return whether the event is paused or not.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.label
documentation: Return the label of the event.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.once
documentation: Return whether the event should only happen once or not.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.priority
documentation: Return the priority of the event.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.next
documentation: Return the next time the event acts.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.step
documentation: Return the time step of the event, which overwrites the simulation time step.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.watchdog
documentation: Return the watchdog of the event, which is a function that is called when the event exceeds its deadline in real-time mode.

methods.name: Akatosh.event.Event.besteffort
documentation: Returns true if the event is set as best-effort
